result.stdout:
Hypar:
  Tools for creating and managing functions on https://hypar.io

Usage:
  Hypar [command]

Commands:
  new                   Create a new Hypar function.
  publish               Publish your function to Hypar.
  delete                Delete a function from Hypar.
  generate-catalog      Generate code for a ContentCatalog.
  generate-types        Generate user element types from the specified url.
  converter             Tools for managing Hypar converters.
  init                  Generate input, output, and function classes from a hypar.json.
  rename                Rename a function.
  sign-in               Sign in to Hypar.
  sign-out              Sign out of Hypar.
  run                   Run the hypar function found in the current directory. Defaults to running the function whenever the function's code or its input change, but can also run across a provided list of input values instead.
  pull                  Pull changes made to your function via the Hypar web UI down to your local hypar.json.
  edit                  Open a browser window to edit your function configuration.
  version, --version    Display version information
  update                Update the current function to the latest version of Hypar, or to a specified version.
  test                  Tools for dealing with tests for Hypar functions.

commands.payload:
Hypar:
  Tools for creating and managing functions on https://hypar.io

Usage:
  Hypar [command]

Commands:
  new                   Create a new Hypar function.
  publish               Publish your function to Hypar.
  delete                Delete a function from Hypar.
  generate-catalog      Generate code for a ContentCatalog.
  generate-types        Generate user element types from the specified url.
  converter             Tools for managing Hypar converters.
  init                  Generate input, output, and function classes from a hypar.json.
  rename                Rename a function.
  sign-in               Sign in to Hypar.
  sign-out              Sign out of Hypar.
  run                   Run the hypar function found in the current directory. Defaults to running the function whenever the function's code or its input change, but can also run across a provided list of input values instead.
  pull                  Pull changes made to your function via the Hypar web UI down to your local hypar.json.
  edit                  Open a browser window to edit your function configuration.
  version, --version    Display version information
  update                Update the current function to the latest version of Hypar, or to a specified version.
  test                  Tools for dealing with tests for Hypar functions.

result.stdout:
generate-catalog:
  Generate code for a ContentCatalog.

Usage:
  Hypar generate-catalog [options]

Options:
  -u, --uri <URI>          The uri of the catalog json file. This can be a relative file path, or a url.
  -o, --output <OUTPUT>    The directory into which the generated files will be written.

commands.payload:
generate-catalog:
  Generate code for a ContentCatalog.

Usage:
  Hypar generate-catalog [options]

Options:
  -u, --uri <URI>          The uri of the catalog json file. This can be a relative file path, or a url.
  -o, --output <OUTPUT>    The directory into which the generated files will be written.

result.stdout:
generate-types:
  Generate user element types from the specified url.

Usage:
  Hypar generate-types [options]

Options:
  -u, --uri <URI>          The uri of the user element schema. This can be a relative file path, or a url.
  -o, --output <OUTPUT>    The directory into which the generated files will be written.
  -t, --tags <TAGS>        Generate types for all schemas with the provided tags.

commands.payload:
generate-types:
  Generate user element types from the specified url.

Usage:
  Hypar generate-types [options]

Options:
  -u, --uri <URI>          The uri of the user element schema. This can be a relative file path, or a url.
  -o, --output <OUTPUT>    The directory into which the generated files will be written.
  -t, --tags <TAGS>        Generate types for all schemas with the provided tags.

result.stdout:
init:
  Generate input, output, and function classes from a hypar.json.

Usage:
  Hypar init [options]

Options:
  -o, --output-path <OUTPUT-PATH>                                     The path to which generated files will be written.
  -t, --tests-path <TESTS-PATH>                                       The path to which generated test files will be written.
  -d, --dependencies-path <DEPENDENCIES-PATH>                         The path to which dependencies will be written.
  -sd, --skip-inspect-dependencies <SKIP-INSPECT-DEPENDENCIES>        Skip inspecting dependencies to check for type collisions with generated types.
  -sv, --skip-function-version-check <SKIP-FUNCTION-VERSION-CHECK>    Skip checking the Hypar.Function version against the latest version on the server.

init:

Usage:

Options:
  -o, --output-path <OUTPUT-PATH>                                     The path to which generated files will be written.
  -t, --tests-path <TESTS-PATH>                                       The path to which generated test files will be written.
  -d, --dependencies-path <DEPENDENCIES-PATH>                         The path to which dependencies will be written.
  -sd, --skip-inspect-dependencies <SKIP-INSPECT-DEPENDENCIES>        Skip inspecting dependencies to check for type collisions with generated types.
  -sv, --skip-function-version-check <SKIP-FUNCTION-VERSION-CHECK>    Skip checking the Hypar.Function version against the latest version on the server.

result.stdout:
new:
  Create a new Hypar function.

Usage:
  Hypar new [options]

Options:
  -n, --name <NAME>                    The name of the newly created function.
  -np, --no-publish <NO-PUBLISH>       Don't automatically call `hypar publish` after creation.
  --python <PYTHON>                    BETA: Use Python for the function instead of C#. This is a pre-release version that we don't suggest using yet.
  --typescript <TYPESCRIPT>            BETA: Use Typescript for the function instead of C#. This is a pre-release version that we don't suggest using yet.
  --list-templates <LIST-TEMPLATES>    List available templates.
  --template <TEMPLATE>                The template to use for the new function. Supply a name or a git repository url. Use `--list-templates` to see available templates.
  --function-id <FUNCTION-ID>          Create a function from an already established record, such as when you're using the function wizard.

commands.payload:
new:
  Create a new Hypar function.

Usage:
  Hypar new [options]

Options:
  -n, --name <NAME>                    The name of the newly created function.
  -np, --no-publish <NO-PUBLISH>       Don't automatically call `hypar publish` after creation.
  --python <PYTHON>                    BETA: Use Python for the function instead of C#. This is a pre-release version that we don't suggest using yet.
  --typescript <TYPESCRIPT>            BETA: Use Typescript for the function instead of C#. This is a pre-release version that we don't suggest using yet.
  --list-templates <LIST-TEMPLATES>    List available templates.
  --template <TEMPLATE>                The template to use for the new function. Supply a name or a git repository url. Use `--list-templates` to see available templates.
  --function-id <FUNCTION-ID>          Create a function from an already established record, such as when you're using the function wizard.

result.stdout:
publish:
  Publish your function to Hypar.

Usage:
  Hypar publish [options]

Options:
  --disable-pull-check <DISABLE-PULL-CHECK>                       Disable the check for newer versions of the function live on Hypar
  -sd, --skip-inspect-dependencies <SKIP-INSPECT-DEPENDENCIES>    Skip inspecting dependencies to check for type collisions with generated types.

result.stdout:
run:
  Run the hypar function found in the current directory. Defaults to running the function whenever the function's code or its input change, but can also run across a provided list of input values instead.

Usage:
  Hypar run [options]

Options:
  --batch-input-file <BATCH-INPUT-FILE>                           An optional JSON file containing an array of input objects for the function. If provided, you must also specify --batch-elements-output-dir or --batch-gltf-output-dir.
  --batch-elements-output-dir <BATCH-ELEMENTS-OUTPUT-DIR>         Where the Hypar Elements model files will be stored, as numbered .json files corresponding to each input in --batch-input-file.
  --batch-gltf-output-dir <BATCH-GLTF-OUTPUT-DIR>                 Where the glTF output will be stored, as numbered .glb files corresponding to each input in --batch-input-file.
  --batch-output-report-file <BATCH-OUTPUT-REPORT-FILE>           Where to write a summary of all execution output parameters and timing, as a JSON file.
  -w, --workflow-id <WORKFLOW-ID>                                 Sync the specified workflow to use as input to the locally running function.
  -sd, --skip-inspect-dependencies <SKIP-INSPECT-DEPENDENCIES>    Skip inspecting dependencies to check for type collisions with generated types.

commands.payload:
run:
  Run the hypar function found in the current directory. Defaults to running the function whenever the function's code or its input change, but can also run across a provided list of input values instead.

Usage:
  Hypar run [options]

Options:
  --batch-input-file <BATCH-INPUT-FILE>                           An optional JSON file containing an array of input objects for the function. If provided, you must also specify --batch-elements-output-dir or --batch-gltf-output-dir.
  --batch-elements-output-dir <BATCH-ELEMENTS-OUTPUT-DIR>         Where the Hypar Elements model files will be stored, as numbered .json files corresponding to each input in --batch-input-file.
  --batch-gltf-output-dir <BATCH-GLTF-OUTPUT-DIR>                 Where the glTF output will be stored, as numbered .glb files corresponding to each input in --batch-input-file.
  --batch-output-report-file <BATCH-OUTPUT-REPORT-FILE>           Where to write a summary of all execution output parameters and timing, as a JSON file.
  -w, --workflow-id <WORKFLOW-ID>                                 Sync the specified workflow to use as input to the locally running function.
  -sd, --skip-inspect-dependencies <SKIP-INSPECT-DEPENDENCIES>    Skip inspecting dependencies to check for type collisions with generated types.

test:
  Tools for dealing with tests for Hypar functions.

Usage:
  Hypar test [command]

Commands:

test:
  Tools for dealing with tests for Hypar functions.

Usage:
  Hypar test [command]

  g, generate    Generate a test for the current function based on the data in a workflow.

result.stdout:
generate:
  Generate a test for the current function based on the data in a workflow.

Usage:
  Hypar test generate [options]

Options:
  -w, --workflow-id <WORKFLOW-ID>      The id of the workflow to use for building the test.
  -n, --name <NAME>                    The name of the test to be created. Default name is "<functionName>Test"
  -m, --model-output <MODEL-OUTPUT>    The model output to use for the test. If not specified, the first found model output will be used.

commands.payload:
generate:
  Generate a test for the current function based on the data in a workflow.

Usage:
  Hypar test generate [options]

Options:
  -w, --workflow-id <WORKFLOW-ID>      The id of the workflow to use for building the test.
  -n, --name <NAME>                    The name of the test to be created. Default name is "<functionName>Test"
  -m, --model-output <MODEL-OUTPUT>    The model output to use for the test. If not specified, the first found model output will be used.

result.stdout:
update:
  Update the current function to the latest version of Hypar, or to a specified version.

Usage:
  Hypar update [options]

Options:
  -v, --version <VERSION>            The version of Hypar to use.
  -pre, --prerelease <PRERELEASE>    Use prerelease versions of Hypar packages.
  -l, --local <LOCAL>                Create local references to the Hypar dependencies.
  --skip-inputs                      Don't upgrade old-style inputs to input_schema

  -l, --local <LOCAL>                Create local references to the Hypar dependencies.
  --skip-inputs                      Don't upgrade old-style inputs to input_schema
